steps.title: Test which point satisfies both inequalities
steps.content: A solution must satisfy both: $y \le x + 6$ AND $y \ge -3x - 2$. Substitute each choice into both inequalities.

steps.title: Test the other choices through C
steps.content:
• Choice A - $\left(-12,0\right)$: $0 \le -12+6$ becomes $0 \le -6$, which is false.
  • Choice B - $\left(0,-12\right)$: $-12 \le 0+6$ is true, but $-12 \ge -3(0)-2$ becomes $-12 \ge -2$, which is false.
  • Choice C - $\left(0,12\right)$: $12 \le 0+6$ becomes $12 \le 6$, which is false.

steps.title: Testing the correct answer (12, 0)
steps.content: First inequality: $0 \le 12 + 6 = 18$? Yes. Second inequality: $0 \ge -3(12) - 2 = -36 - 2 = -38$? Yes. Both inequalities are satisfied. D is correct.
